title: alchemilla plicatula subsp. fontqueri
intro: Alchemilla plicatula subsp. fontqueri, also known as the curled alchemilla, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its pinnate leaves and yellowish-green flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Alchemilla plicatula subsp. fontqueri include its ability to reduce swelling, promote healing, and soothe digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in salves and creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
